description:
You are a DevOps engineer who has been asked to quickly triage a small
    data-pipeline log so the on-call staff can decide if an automatic recovery
    should be triggered.

    1. The log file already exists on the system at  
       /home/user/app/logs/pipeline.log

    2. Your goal is to create a **single, plain-text summary file** at  
       /home/user/app/reports/log_summary.txt  

       The file **must contain exactly three lines, in this order,
       each terminated by a single UNIX newline (LF):**

       • Line 1 → `ERROR_COUNT:<integer>`  
       • Line 2 → `WARNING_COUNT:<integer>`  
       • Line 3 → `LAST_ERROR_TIMESTAMP:<YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS>`  

       Notes on the required format that the automated tests will check:
       • No leading or trailing spaces anywhere on any of the three lines.  
       • The colons shown above are literal.  
       • `<integer>` is the total number of matching log lines that contain
         the exact substring “ERROR” (case-sensitive).  
       • `<integer>` for warnings is the total number of matching log lines that contain
         the exact substring “WARNING” (case-sensitive).  
       • `<YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS>` must be copied verbatim from the timestamp
         portion of the *last* log line in the file that contains
         the substring “ERROR”.  
       • Be sure to end the final line with a newline as well, so the file
         contains three newlines in total.

    3. Keep it simple: you should be able to produce the summary with
       standard GNU/Linux command-line utilities such as grep, awk, wc, tail,
       head, cut, etc.  A typical solution should not need more than
       three to four individual shell commands in total.

    4. Do **not** modify the original pipeline.log file.

    5. Place the summary file only after you have verified that the counts and
       timestamp are correct, because the automated grader will read
       /home/user/app/reports/log_summary.txt verbatim.

    When you are done, the grader will look only for the presence and exact
    contents of /home/user/app/reports/log_summary.txt as described above.
truth:
<!-- Static files that already exist before the agent begins -->
    <preexisting_file path="/home/user/app/logs/pipeline.log"><![CDATA[
[2023-09-01 10:00:00] INFO Starting pipeline
[2023-09-01 10:00:01] WARNING Missing optional config value 'threshold', default applied
[2023-09-01 10:00:02] ERROR Failed to connect to database
[2023-09-01 10:00:03] INFO Retrying in 5 seconds
[2023-09-01 10:00:08] INFO Connected after 1 retries
[2023-09-01 10:00:09] WARNING Deprecated API call 'fetch_data_v1'
[2023-09-01 10:00:10] INFO Pipeline finished successfully
]]></preexisting_file>

    <!-- What the agent must create -->
    <expected_file path="/home/user/app/reports/log_summary.txt"><![CDATA[
ERROR_COUNT:1
WARNING_COUNT:2
LAST_ERROR_TIMESTAMP:2023-09-01 10:00:02
]]></expected_file>
